channel.title: Posts on COMP3222 Musings
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on COMP3222 Musings

item.title: Counters
item.description: Counters Counters operate by taking the output of a flip-flop as its clock input

item.title: Registers
item.description: Registers A register is a collection of flip-flop circuits.

item.title: Latches and Flip-Flops
item.description: A hypothetical "transmission gate" When active (high), a signal can pass through the gate.

item.title: VHDL Tricks
item.description: VHDL Packages Read here

item.title: Decoders and Encoders
item.description: Decoders and Encoders Decoders Circuits used to decode an $n$-input signal into $2^n$ outputs

item.title: Shannon's Expansion Theorem
item.description: Shannon's Expansion Theorem Mux implementations of logic functions require that a given function be decomposed with respect to the variables that are used as the select inputs

item.title: Applications of Multiplexers
item.description: Practical Applications of Muxers $n \times k$ crossbar switch n inputs, k outputs, connect any input to any output

item.title: Snippet Info
item.description: L1P2 - 4-bit 2-mux L1P3 - 2-bit 3-mux L2P1 - 4-bit number to HEX display (with unhandled values) (bcd_to_hex) L2P2 - 4-bit to 2x HEX L2P3 - bit adder with carry (full_add)

item.title: Lab 2
item.description: Part 1 Truth Table Karnaugh Maps $H_0$ $H_1$ $H_2$ $H_3$ $H_4$ $H_5$ $H_6$ Part 2 Truth Table Annotations Circuit A Truth Table Circuit A Karnaugh Map $A_2$ $A_1$ $A_0$ Logic Function for $z$ $z = 1$ when $V \ge 10$

item.title: VHDL Packages
item.description: A VHDL file can have a PACKAGE declaration which allows a project to be reused across multiple projects

item.title: Arithmetic Circuits
item.description: Binary Addition Half-Adder Circuit Full-Adder Circuit Truth Table $s_i$ Karnaugh Map $c_{i+1}$ Karnaugh Map Circuit Symbol Decomposition Example n-bit Ripply-Carry Adder Subtraction Subtraction is the addition of the two's complement

item.title: Example Circuit Simplification
item.description: Deriving the output function

item.title: NAND and NOR Gates
item.description: NAND and NOR gates are preferred over AND/OR/NOT gates as they require less transistors to implement

item.title: Lab 1
item.description: Part 4 Truth Table

item.title: Altera DE1 Board
item.description: 7 segment LED Accessible via HEX0, HEX1, HEX2, HEX3.

item.title: Karnaugh Maps
item.description: A diagrammatic way to simplify functions by eliminating terms.

item.title: Intro to FPGA
item.description: There are fixed and programmable FPGA devices.

item.title: Introduction to VHDL
item.description: Originally circuit diagrams were used to describe the implementation of the circuit.

item.title: Introduction to Synthesis
item.description: Name Expression Gate Logical AND $ x_1 \cdot x_2 $ Logical OR $ x_1 + x_2 $ NOT $ \overline{x} $ Truth Table A truth table is a mathematical table used in logic-specifically in connection with Boolean algebra, boolean functions, and propositional calculus-which sets out the functional values of logical expressions on each of their functional arguments, that is, for each combination of values taken by their logical variables.

item.title: Intro to Quartus
item.description: Notes EDA - Electronic Design Automation Altera Quartus (now owned by Intel) Hardware Altera DE1 Don't need the 7.
